citations.description: Directory listing of src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ shows: __init__.py, attest.py, coach_moves.py, criteria_routing.json, det_floor.py, det_invariants.py, det_lint.py, det_operator_attested.py, fidelity_spot_eval.py, manifest.py, orchestrator.py, pass1.py, passes.py, production_batch_runner.py, registry.py, resign.py, sidecar.py, sizing.py, workflow_ops.py — no drift_floor.py.
search_files query 'drift_floor' across src/ returned no matches.
search_files query 'drift_floor_drop' returned no matches anywhere in the repository.
Directory listing of tests/unit/ shows no test_plan_review_drift_floor.py file.
Full content of decide.py inspected — no drift_floor_drop function or any drift-floor logic present.

findings.criterion: Pass-3 convergence floor for drift-escalated unchanged-plan re-review (LLM-surface work): src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py (the Pass-3 DRIFT floor module), the deterministic drop predicate rebar.llm.review_kernel.decide.drift_floor_drop, and tests/unit/test_plan_review_drift_floor.py must exist in the repository.
findings.detail: None of the three core artefacts claimed by the ticket comment exist in the repository. `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py` is absent from the directory listing of src/rebar/llm/plan_review/. A search for 'drift_floor' across the entire src/ tree returns zero matches. A search for 'drift_floor_drop' likewise returns zero matches — the symbol does not appear anywhere in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py or elsewhere. `tests/unit/test_plan_review_drift_floor.py` is absent from the tests/unit/ directory listing. The ticket comment attesting that commit d5733dbc9 landed these files is not corroborated by any file-tool evidence; the code is simply not present.
findings.remediation: Implement the described drift-floor: create src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py with the Pass-3 DRIFT floor for the plan-UNCHANGED + code-DRIFTED axis, add the drift_floor_drop predicate to src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py, add the required behavioural eval (or confirm plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml covers the drift axis), and add tests/unit/test_plan_review_drift_floor.py. Then update the ticket with the actual commit/change reference.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
